specificity.note: Names the exact instrument (Reg. 2024/2847), both deadlines (11 Sep 2026, 11 Dec 2027), the affected nodes (serviceableFactor, obtainableFactor) with quantified perturbations (×0.85, set 0.02), and the buyer segment (cust.operator-large) plus named operator portals in indicators.

nonObviousness.note: The narrative shows the model's cert stack (EN 50600, ecodesign 2019/424, CE/TÜV) omits the CRA entirely, and whyMissable gives a real hiding mechanism — the Dec-2027 headline date sits outside the 12-month window while procurement clocks run ahead. Falls short of 4 because 'buyers front-run compliance deadlines' is a fairly standard regulatory-risk pattern a diligent founder tracking EU product law might surface.

mechanismDepth.note: Trigger and model-impact steps are well-referenced, but the propagation link — notified-body scarcity screening out the entrant — collapses under the risk's own contradicting stances showing ~90% of products are Default class with self-assessment, and the rationale admits 'no direct evidence 2026 RFPs already carry CRA clauses'. A hostile 'why?' at the propagation arrow is not survived as written.

evidenceQuality.note: The trigger (dates, product scope) has ≥2 independent corroborating sources (Cloudsmith, norppa, craevidence), but the severity-bearing premise (notified-body route) is directly contradicted by two attached sources; the risk honestly marks itself 'contested' and revised likelihood from 0.45 to 0.38. Honest handling saves it from lower, but a load-bearing premise stands contradicted.

decisionRelevance.note: Mitigations are next-two-quarter actions (CRA classification gap assessment, SBOM/SDL standup, OEM-wrapper sequencing of first sales) that change spend and channel sequencing, and the indicators (TED notices, OJEU hEN listings with thresholds) are concretely monitorable.
